sections.paragraph:
ὑάκινθος, ου, ὁ (s. prec. entry; as early as Hom. as the name of a flower) the jacinth or hyacinth (Peripl. Eryth. 56 [gender undetermined]; Galen XIII 970; Ptolem. 7, 4, 1; Heliod. 2, 30, 3 [in him clearly fem]; Achilles Tat. 2, 11, 3 [gender undetermined]; the λίθος ἡλίου Cat. Cod. Astr. IX/2 p. 152, 18.—In the LXX; TestSol 21:2; JosAs 2:15 al. and in Philo and Joseph. [Ant. 3, 164] hyacinth-colored cloth is meant), a precious stone Rv 21:20, perh. blue in color (Michel Psellus, in Lapidaires p. 203, ln. 79f θαλαττόχρω ‘sea-hued’), someth. like the sapphire (but s. vs. 19); on it s. OED s.v. hyacinth 1ab. It was often made into gems by the ancients (Pliny, NH 37, 9, 41f).—For lit. see s.v. ἀμέθυστος.—DELG. M-M.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

Heliod Heliod , III? a.d.—List 5

Achilles Tat Achilles Tat , IV a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

perh. perh. = perhaps

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

Psellus Psellus , History, XI a.d.—List 5

  1. ln. = line

someth. someth. = something

OED OED = Oxford English Dictionary—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1022
